---
title: "Duojiang | Plant Ecophysiology Research"
page-layout: full
---

:::{.hero}
## Duojiang He

**Plant Ecophysiology Researcher** studying how plants sense, respond to, and survive environmental stress.

I investigate the physiological mechanisms underlying drought tolerance, hydraulic failure, and photosynthetic adaptation across species and ecosystems. My work combines field observations, controlled experiments, and ecophysiological measurements to bridge the gap between cellular processes and ecosystem function.

::: {.hero-meta}
::: {.hero-chip}
Focus
Plant water relations, drought physiology, and photosynthetic stress responses
:::
::: {.hero-chip}
Methods
Gas exchange, chlorophyll fluorescence, hydraulic measurements, xylem imaging
:::
::: {.hero-chip}
Systems
Diverse plant species across gradients of water availability
:::
:::
:::

## Get in touch

:::{.card-grid}
::: {.panel}
### Email

[jiangduo2013@gmail.com](mailto:jiangduo2013@gmail.com)
:::
::: {.panel}
### GitHub

[@duojianghuji](https://github.com/duojianghuji)
:::
::: {.panel}
### ORCID

[0000-0000-0000-0000](https://orcid.org/0000-0000-0000-0000)
:::
:::

## Research highlights

:::{.card-grid}
::: {.panel}
### Research themes

Organized around core questions: plant hydraulic failure, photosynthetic acclimation, and root-soil interactions under stress. [Explore →](/research.qmd)
:::
::: {.panel}
### Publications

Papers, preprints, and conference presentations with plain-language summaries explaining the significance of each output. [View all →](/publications.qmd)
:::
::: {.panel}
### Active projects

Current work on stomatal regulation, xylem vulnerability, and species coexistence under drought. [See projects →](/projects.qmd)
:::
:::

## More

- **[Resources](/resources.qmd)** — Code, protocols, and field notes
- **[Field Notes →](https://duojianghuji.github.io/duojiang-field-notes/)** — Observations and methods from the field (external site)
